description: Coverage proves execution, not correctness of assertions.
body:
High coverage can coexist with weak assertions. Use coverage as a map to untested code, then ensure tests assert meaningful behavior and negative paths.

Evidence boundary: apply only when current task, repository, or validation evidence matches this pack boundary. Do not override a more specific pack or explicit project contract. Use when Code Testing work touches test runner output, fixture intent, coverage boundaries, flake history, and negative paths. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include focused test output, regression tests, fixture assertions, and rerun evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves testing implementation details only, masking flakes, and claiming coverage without behavior proof, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
